prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_complaint_record investigation_1)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint investigation_1 investigator_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(flag_product_for_validation investigation_1 product_batch_1 complainant_contact_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record investigation_1 reviewer_1)
(mark_investigation_ready_for_change_package investigation_1 product_batch_1 reviewer_1)
(release_reviewer_from_record investigation_1 reviewer_1)
(release_complainant_contact investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_complaint_record capa_1)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint capa_1 investigator_2)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(flag_process_for_validation capa_1 process_line_1 complainant_contact_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record capa_1 reviewer_1)
(assign_reviewer_and_mark_capa_ready_for_package capa_1 process_line_1 reviewer_1)
(release_reviewer_from_record capa_1 reviewer_1)
(assemble_change_control_package investigation_1 capa_1 product_batch_1 process_line_1 change_control_package_1)
(release_complainant_contact capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(mark_change_control_package_validation_ready change_control_package_1 investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(finalize_investigation_record investigation_1 change_control_package_1)
(finalize_capa_record capa_1 change_control_package_1)
(register_complaint_record casefile_1)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint casefile_1 investigator_3)
(release_complainant_contact investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_equipment_to_casefile casefile_1 equipment_1)
(link_validation_specialist_to_casefile casefile_1 validation_specialist_1)
(attach_validation_protocol_to_casefile_and_package casefile_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1)
(complete_technical_review_without_validation_dependency casefile_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1 complainant_contact_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record casefile_1 reviewer_1)
(initiate_validation_specialist_workflow casefile_1 equipment_1 reviewer_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1)
(validation_specialist_complete_casefile_review casefile_1 validation_specialist_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1)
(finalize_casefile_without_release_authority casefile_1)
(release_reviewer_from_record casefile_1 reviewer_1)
(unlink_equipment_from_casefile casefile_1 equipment_1)
(unlink_validation_specialist_from_casefile casefile_1 validation_specialist_1)
(release_complainant_contact casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint investigation_1 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_investigation_in_quality_system investigation_1 investigator_1 regulatory_reference_1)
(register_complaint_record casefile_2)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint casefile_2 investigator_1)
(release_complainant_contact investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(link_equipment_to_casefile casefile_2 equipment_1)
(link_validation_specialist_to_casefile casefile_2 validation_specialist_1)
(attach_validation_protocol_to_casefile_and_package casefile_2 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1)
(complete_technical_review_without_validation_dependency casefile_2 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1 complainant_contact_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record casefile_2 reviewer_1)
(initiate_validation_specialist_workflow casefile_2 equipment_1 reviewer_1 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1)
(validation_specialist_complete_casefile_review casefile_2 validation_specialist_1 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1)
(finalize_casefile_without_release_authority casefile_2)
(release_complainant_contact casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint capa_1 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_capa_in_quality_system capa_1 investigator_2 regulatory_reference_1)
(release_complainant_contact capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint casefile_1 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_casefile_in_quality_system casefile_1 investigator_3 regulatory_reference_1)
(release_complainant_contact casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint casefile_2 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_casefile_in_quality_system casefile_2 investigator_1 regulatory_reference_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for a pharmaceutics complaint to quality system scenario. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Task  
- The problem involves creating a planning solution in PDDL for a pharmaceutics complaint scenario related to a quality system.  
- The goal is to output an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, formatted with one action per line.  
- Each action must follow the structure (action-name arg1 arg2 ...), ensuring clarity and adherence to PDDL standards.

## Exploring Record Relationships  
- Complaint_record, investigation_record, capa_record, and quality_case_file share inheritance, all stemming from complaint_record predicates.  
- Casefile_1 and casefile_2 are quality_case_file types, linking back to the complaint record structure.  

## Checking Goal Requirements  
- The goals are to register investigation_1, capa_1, casefile_1, and casefile_2 in the quality system, each needing specific prerequisites.  
- Casefile_2 is linked to supplier_2, which isn’t available, unlike supplier_1, potentially complicating registration.  

## Planning Initial Actions  
- Start by registering all records using register_complaint_record, beginning with investigation_1 and others like capa_1.  
- After registration, assign investigators (three available) and link complainant_contact_1, noting only one complainant is available.  

## Handling Complainant Availability  
- Linking complainant_contact_1 to records uses link_complainant_contact_to_complaint, making it unavailable afterward (not complainant_contact_available).  
- To link to multiple records, release_complainant_contact is needed, which requires record_linked_complainant_contact first, suggesting a cycle of linking and releasing.  

## Assessing Record Finalization  
- Finalizing records, like investigation_record, involves multiple steps including finalize_investigation_record, needing investigation_ready and other conditions.  
- Registration in the quality system requires record_regulatory_associated, linked via link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint, which needs finalized records and complainant contact linkage.

## Assessing Resource Availability  
- Only one complainant_contact_1 and reviewer_1 are available, requiring sharing across multiple records via linking and releasing.  
- Resources like supp_doc_1, equipment_1, val_specialist_1, and regulatory_ref are singular, while investigators (3), val_prot (2), risk_assess (2), and release_auth (2) are more plentiful.  

## Examining Casefile Differences  
- Casefile_1 links to available supplier_1, while casefile_2 links to unavailable supplier_2, potentially blocking supplier review for casefile_2.  
- Both casefiles include inv_1, capa_1, and ccp, but differ in val_prot: casefile_1 uses vp1, casefile_2 uses vp2.  

## Planning Record Finalization  
- Finalizing investigation_1 and capa_1 likely requires compiling ccp and ensuring validation readiness, impacting casefile technical reviews.  
- Registration in the quality system needs finalized records, linked complainant, and regulatory_ref, necessitating a cycle of linking and releasing due to limited resources.  

## Considering Investigation Path  
- For investigation_1, flagging for validation requires an open record, linked complainant, and impacted product, with effects marking it for validation.  
- Assigning a reviewer may be needed later to set ready_for_change_package, depending on the chosen path.

## Managing Complainant Contact  
- Releasing complainant_contact_1 from investigation_1 makes it available for linking to capa_1, enabling further actions like flagging for validation.  
- Linking complainant_contact_1 to capa_1 requires prior release, ensuring resource availability for subsequent steps like triage completion.  

## Assigning Reviewers  
- Assigning reviewer_1 to capa_1 after releasing from investigation_1 prepares for marking capa_ready_for_change_package, requiring prior reviewer assignment.  
- The action assign_reviewer_and_mark_capa_ready_for_package uses an already assigned reviewer, indicating a naming misalign but functionally correct for readiness.  

## Preparing for Change Package  
- Both investigation_1 and capa_1 are marked ready for change control package after validation and reviewer assignment, aligning with process and product validation.  
- Assembling the change control package for investigation_1 and capa_1 compiles necessary elements, setting the stage for further casefile processing.

## Assessing Investigator Availability  
- Investigator_3 is currently assigned to casefile_1, limiting availability for casefile_2.  
- Investigators are only released upon registering in the quality system, creating a bottleneck for casefile_2.  

## Evaluating Casefile Processing  
- Casefile_2 registration requires an available investigator, but all three (investigator_1, 2, 3) are occupied with other records.  
- Processing investigation_1 and capa_1 first may free up investigators, but requires careful sequencing to manage resource constraints.  

## Exploring Registration Strategy  
- Registering some records early in the quality system could release investigators, but finalization and regulatory association are needed first.  
- Sequencing tasks, such as finalizing investigation_1 and capa_1 before casefiles, may optimize investigator availability for casefile_2.

## Sequencing Record Processing  
- Process investigation_1, capa_1, and casefile_1 to finalized state using investigators 1, 2, and 3, ensuring resources are shared as needed.  
- After finalizing, move complainant to investigation_1, link regulatory reference, and register to free investigator_1 for casefile_2.  

## Managing Resource Availability  
- Unlink equipment and validation specialist from casefile_1 after completion to make them available for casefile_2 processing.  
- Release reviewer from casefile_1 before starting casefile_2 to ensure resource availability for subsequent tasks.  

## Finalizing Casefile_2  
- Assign freed investigator_1 to casefile_2, link complainant, and complete the workflow, including equipment and reviewer sharing.  
- Register casefile_2 after linking regulatory reference, freeing investigator_1 and completing all four records.

## Managing Resource Availability  
- Releasing complainant contact from investigation_1 after linking ensures it remains available for casefile_2, maintaining workflow continuity.  
- Linking equipment and validation specialist to casefile_2, along with validation protocol, sets up technical review without dependency issues.  

## Finalizing Casefile_2  
- Assigning reviewer_1 and initiating validation workflow for casefile_2 moves processing forward, ensuring all prerequisites are met.  
- Finalizing casefile_2 without release authority completes its processing, aligning with earlier finalized records.  

## Registering Remaining Records  
- Releasing complainant contact from casefile_2 and linking to capa_1 enables registration, freeing investigator_2 in the quality system.  
- Similarly, processing casefile_1 and re-linking resources allows registration, freeing investigator_3 and completing the sequence.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
